treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 400 mg bid orally initially, later reduced to 200 mg bid
treatment_information.treatment_response: Well-tolerated, not interacting with the immunosuppressive regimen, maintained albendazole sulfoxide plasma levels within target range (2.30 to 3.30 µmol/L)

Pathological examination confirmed the diagnosis of AE, showing an unequivocal pseudotumoral lesion with numerous small vesicles and ill-defined borders, multiple areas of necrosis and granuloma surrounding slender parasitic PAS-positive layers

treatment_timeline.event: Treated with intravenous methylprednisolone for persistent acute cellular rejection
Required basiliximab for persistent acute cellular rejection
Irregular hepatomegaly detected associated with persisting pain in the right hypochondrium
Abdominal scan revealed a 44 mm lesion in the hepatic segment IV with satellite nodules and necrotic adenomegaly suggesting cholangiocarcinoma
Diagnosis of AE confirmed via MRI, PET/CT scan, liver biopsy, and serological tests
Treatment by albendazole (400 mg bid orally) initiated
Patient asymptomatic with unremarkable clinical examination and imaging, undetectable or weak and declining serology levels

patient_outcome.final_status: Asymptomatic with unremarkable clinical examination and imaging
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Regular monitoring of albendazole sulfoxide plasma levels, close clinical, serological and radiological post-intervention follow-up required
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Close clinical, serological and radiological post-intervention follow-up will remain essential; even after the end of treatment planned for two years post-alveolar echinococcosis surgery at the earliest, or until the serology becomes negative
